note: 上流マクロの文脈。WIRED 風の編集フィード。事実の英文要約のみ（記事リンクなし）。第三者の予測・決定は事実として記す（JIN 自身の予測・確率ではない）。運用者が手で差し込む。

items.headline: Europe's central banks tilt hawkish as energy costs bite
items.summary: June rate decisions skewed toward tightening on Iran-driven energy prices. The ECB raised to 2.25% and the Bank of Japan to 1.00%; the Fed held at 3.5–3.75%, with the BOE (3.75%), SNB (0.00%) and Norges Bank (4.25%) on hold. The Czech National Bank hiked for the first time in four years.

items.headline: Households pile into corporate bonds as yields climb
items.summary: After the BOJ moved its policy rate to 1%, household interest income reached a record. Retail corporate-bond issuance rose 14% year on year to an all-time high, with heavy demand for SoftBank Group and Rakuten Group paper — even as younger borrowers face steeper mortgage and auto-loan costs.

items.headline: A 'smaller central bank': the Warsh Fed gets going
items.summary: Under new chair Kevin Warsh, the Fed leans toward balance-sheet runoff and simpler communication, and more FOMC participants now pencil in hikes. The yen briefly touched 161 to the dollar, a two-year low, on broad dollar strength.

items.headline: Nikkei tops ¥70,000 as gains concentrate in a few names
items.summary: Total market value is up ¥218tn year to date, more than half of it from a 'Japanese M7' — Kioxia, SoftBank, Advantest, Tokyo Electron, Murata, Shin-Etsu and Hitachi. Semiconductor names led (Kioxia ×9.3, Taiyo Yuden ×5.6) while domestic-demand stocks lagged (Aeon −46%, Nexon −42%, Nintendo −32%).

items.headline: Indonesia and the Philippines hike to slow currency slides
items.summary: Oil-driven inflation and weak currencies prompted back-to-back rate increases across Southeast Asia, and Singapore tightened for the first time in three and a half years. Separately, BYD was reported as 2025's top global automaker, ahead of Toyota and Volkswagen.

items.headline: The 'bean-free coffee' race ignites over supply risk
items.summary: Climate-driven supply concerns are pushing alternative coffee into the mainstream. Coca-Cola Japan's CAFÉ WATER (¥149 / 500ml) and Asahi's planned Mirai no BLACK (~¥227 / 430ml) follow US startup Atomo Coffee. Japan is the world's fourth-largest coffee consumer.

items.headline: US softens its Iran terms to secure a deal
items.summary: A draft memorandum shows US concessions on enrichment (in-country dilution), the Strait of Hormuz (60 days of free passage) and oil sanctions, with final terms deferred — drawing discontent from parts of the governing party.

items.headline: Big managers weigh bundled alternative-asset funds
items.summary: Nomura, Nissay and peers are studying retail funds that bundle overseas infrastructure and private equity into a single diversified product, after the Investment Trusts Association revised its rules in June. Low liquidity and disclosure remain the open questions.

items.headline: Institutional crypto gains a foothold in Japan
items.summary: A corporate pension fund in Okayama plans to begin crypto investing within FY2026, citing near-zero correlation between bitcoin and the dollar as a diversifier. An EY-Parthenon survey found 73% of global institutions had invested by 2025.

items.headline: Forward-deployed engineers become tech's hottest hire
items.summary: Salesforce, Google, Anthropic and OpenAI are scaling up hiring and training of forward-deployed engineers. Google is putting $750m into partner workforce development, Anthropic is forming a staffing venture with Blackstone, and OpenAI set up a new company with TPG and acquired UK-based Tomoro AI.
